content:
3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

1. Introduction

• What you will build

• Why FastAPI is beginner-friendly

• What readers will learn in this chapter
Example:
In this chapter, we’ll build our first FastAPI application from scratch. By the end of this tutorial, you’ll know how to install FastAPI, create your first API, run the development server, and test the API using Swagger UI.
2. Prerequisites
Mention what users should have installed.

• Python 3.10+

• VS Code or PyCharm

• pip

• Terminal
3. Create a Project Folder
mkdir fastapi-tutorial
cd fastapi-tutorialExplain why we create a dedicated project folder.
4. Create a Virtual Environment
Windows
python -m venv venvLinux/macOS
python3 -m venv venvExplain:

• Why virtual environments are important

• They isolate project dependencies
5. Activate Virtual Environment
Windows
venv\Scripts\activateLinux/macOS
source venv/bin/activateShow what the terminal looks like after activation.

6. Install FastAPI
pip install fastapiExplain what FastAPI is.
7. Install Uvicorn
pip install uvicornExplain:

• FastAPI is the framework.

• Uvicorn is the ASGI server that runs the application.
8. Verify Installation
pip listReaders should see:

• fastapi

• uvicorn
9. Create main.py
Project structure:
fastapi-tutorial/
│── venv/
│── main.pyExplain why main.py is commonly used as the entry point.
10. Write Your First FastAPI Code
from fastapi import FastAPIapp = FastAPI()
@app.get("/")
def home():
return {"message": "Welcome to FastAPI"}
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Explain every line:

• import FastAPI

• create app

• decorator

• route

• return JSON
11. Run the Server
uvicorn main:app --reloadExplain:

• main

• app

• --reload
12. Open in Browser
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Visit:
http://127.0.0.1:8000
Output:
Press enter or click to view image in full size

13. Swagger Documentation
Visit:
http://127.0.0.1:8000/docsPress enter or click to view image in full size

Explain:

• Automatic API documentation

• Interactive testing

• No Postman required for simple APIs
14. ReDoc Documentation
Visit:
http://127.0.0.1:8000/redocExplain the difference between Swagger UI and ReDoc.
15. How FastAPI Works
Simple flow diagram:
Browser ↓
HTTP Request
↓
Uvicorn Server
↓
FastAPI
↓
Route Function
↓
JSON Response
↓
Browser
16. Common Errors
Examples:
Module not found
ModuleNotFoundError
Solution:
pip install fastapiUvicorn not found
uvicorn: command not found
Solution:
pip install uvicornAddress already in use
Port 8000 already in useSolution:
Use another port:
uvicorn main:app --reload --port 800117. Summary
Recap what readers learned:

• Installed FastAPI

• Installed Uvicorn

• Created a project

• Wrote the first API

• Ran the server

• Tested the API

• Used Swagger UI

• Used ReDoc
18. What’s Next?
End the article with a preview:
In Chapter 03, we’ll learn Path Parameters in FastAPI and build dynamic APIs that accept values directly from the URL.
This structure is beginner-friendly, covers all essential concepts, and is ideal for a Medium tutorial series.
